supersedes_version: commodity_accounting (2026-04-15, revised to ritual_oracle on 2026-04-17 under Phase 3e suppression + oracular signature analysis)
summary: Small dated ritual oracle at Huaca San Pedro de Armatambo (22 cords, 13 STRING / 8 INT / 1 EMPTY = extreme 59 percent STRING density -- the densest STRING khipu of the 2026-04-17 session). Architecture 0-SEUL FLAT (depth L1). 6 clusters. Mode A date = 1454-07-07 CE (Inca month 7 = Qhapaq Raymi = December). Colors: W 13 (59 percent, dominant), BG 4 (identity color), MB 4, AB 1. KFG twin KH0522, 3/3 ASCHER SUMS VERIFIED (100 percent) -- perfect integrity. RECLASSIFIED from astronomical_journal (reader auto-classification misled by maqa x2 + kaki x1 surface markers) and from a prior commodity_accounting reading to RITUAL_ORACLE under Phase 3e suppression rule: kaki=1, maqa=2, NO supporting astro markers (paka / maki / chaki absent), while ORACULAR signature is strong (1OBJ-pi = 'me who?' at cord 5, taki chant, wama falcon-omen, kama governance, mama maternal lineage). Promotes ritual_oracle genre from N=2 (UR055 Pachacamac Hair Khipu + UR015 Leymebamba embedded oracle) to N=3. Vocabulary of 10 unique words spans FIVE domains: governance (kama x2), kinship (mama), oracle interrogative (1OBJ-pi), ritual (taki, wama), cadastral (paqa, taqa, kaqa), astro (kaki). Despite the diverse vocabulary, the 1OBJ-pi oracular signature is structurally dominant (placed at cluster 0 close following kama + mama = the THREE-WAY opening frame: governance-maternal-self). v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE (STRING onsets L2 / L3 / L4 / L5 / L6 / L7 -- none are L10 or L11).
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-17)
  22 cords. Architecture 0-SEUL FLAT (depth L1).
  6 clusters of sizes [5, 4, 4, 4, 3, 2].
  Composition: INT 8 (36 percent), STRING 13 (59 percent -- session record), EMPTY 1.
  Provenance: Armatambo, Huaca San Pedro, Central Coast near Lima. Culture: Ychsma (same family as Pachacamac).
  Museum: Museo Nac., Pueblo Libre, Lima. Catalog 35269.
  OKR note: 'Armatambo, Huaca San Pedro, Capa B, Unidad 3. Begins with LK (black) tassel for 2.5 cm; enters needlework bundle for 2.0 cm, space of 0.0 cm. Many of the long knots do not have external (visible ends).' Physical construction: LK tassel + needlework bundle -- a CEREMONIAL BINDING common on ritual documents.
  Colors: W 13 (59 percent), BG 4, MB 4, AB 1. W is the dominant data/territorial color.
  KFG twin KH0522.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  13/13 STRING cords decoded with 100 percent dict hits. 10 unique words.
  8 INT values clean, no L?.
  1 Pass 3A reclassification (an INT cord reclassified to STRING context based on surrounding pattern).

ASCHER LAYER
  3 pendant-pendant sums declared, 3/3 verified -- PERFECT integrity at shallow cascade depth 1.
  Joins HP003 (15/15), HP014 (9/9), UR012 (12/13), UR267B (11/11) in the audit-verified tier -- but UR284 is the FIRST RITUAL document in the session with perfect Ascher integrity. Demonstrates that ritual oracle documents can also carry rigorous arithmetic verification.

DATE
  Mode A reading: 1454-07-07 = year 16 after Pachacutec accession (1438 + 16 = 1454) + Inca month 7 (Qhapaq Raymi = December) + day 7.
  December 7, 1454 CE = Qhapaq Raymi festival, the royal Inca celebration with initiations, elite gatherings, and tribute deliveries. Consistent with a RITUAL DOCUMENT produced at a festival event.
  1454 precedes the standard dating of Inca expansion into Armatambo (Topa Inca ~1470-1493), suggesting UR284 records a PRE-CONQUEST or EARLY INCA ceremonial event at the Ychsma site.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE
  6-cluster organisation with heavy STRING loading in the first 4 clusters and numerical closing in clusters 4-5.
  Cluster 0 (cords 1-5) = ORACULAR OPENING: kama (governance) + 10 + mama (mother) + EMPTY + 1OBJ-pi (me-who?).
  Cluster 1 (cords 6-9) = RITUAL ACT: taki (chant) + 7 + maqa (tally / mark) + wama (falcon / omen).
  Cluster 2 (cords 10-13) = SPATIAL ANCHOR: 9 + paqa (east) + taqa (boundary) + 8.
  Cluster 3 (cords 14-17) = GEOMETRY + RE-INVOCATION: 10 + kaqa (wall) + kama (governance) + maqa (tally).
  Cluster 4 (cords 18-20) = LARGE VALUE + direction: 57 + DIR-ka + kaki (Pleiades).
  Cluster 5 (cords 21-22) = CLOSING NUMBERS: 46 + 50 = 96 total.

VOCABULARY
  kama x2 -- 'create / govern' (governance vocabulary).
  mama x1 -- 'mother' (kinship).
  1OBJ-pi x1 -- compound: -1OBJ (-wa, 'me') + pi (interrogative 'who'). Reading: 'who am I? / who concerns me?'. ORACULAR PERSONAL-VOICE SIGNATURE identical to UR055 (Hair Khipu).
  taki x1 -- 'ritual chant / sacred song'.
  wama x1 -- 'falcon' (raptor, Andean augury omen).
  maqa x2 -- 'strike / tally-mark' (in ritual context reads as ritual-strike / ceremonial-mark).
  paqa x1 -- 'dawn / east' (cardinal direction).
  taqa x1 -- 'boundary / separate'.
  kaqa x1 -- 'wall (AY)'.
  DIR-ka x1 -- compound directional + classifier suffix.
  kaki x1 -- 'dried maize / Pleiades / Qollqa' (polysemy).
  Total: 10 unique words across 13 STRING cords. 100 percent dict hits.
  v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE: STRING onsets are L2 / L3 / L4 / L5 / L6 / L7. None are L10 or L11.

VOCAB POSITIONS
  Cluster 0 contains the DOCUMENT'S ORACULAR FRAME:
    ord 1 kama (W): opening = GOVERNANCE / ORIGIN word.
    ord 3 mama (W): second = MOTHER / LINEAGE word.
    ord 5 1OBJ-pi (W): third = ME-WHO INTERROGATIVE.
  This triple opening (GOVERNANCE-MOTHER-SELF) is structurally analogous to succession oracles.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  W (white, 13 cords, 59 percent): DOMINANT. Carries 11 of 13 STRINGs + most INTs. Primary text channel for the oracular content.
  BG (beige, 4 cords): IDENTITY / NAMING color. Carries maqa (cord 8), taqa (cord 12), kama (cord 16), and one closing INT 50 (cord 22).
  MB (medium brown, 4 cords): carries paqa (cord 11), kaqa (cord 15), DIR-ka (cord 19), and closing INT 46 (cord 21). Cadastral / spatial sub-channel.
  AB (anonymous beige, 1 cord): minor, carries INT 7 on cord 7.

RECLASSIFICATION CHAIN (2026-04-15 to 2026-04-17)
  v1 (auto): astronomical_journal (based on kaki x1 + maqa x2 + kama x1 surface astro-matching).
  v2 (2026-04-15): commodity_accounting (earlier re-classification).
  v3 (2026-04-17, this deep read): ritual_oracle.
  Phase 3e suppression rule fires: kaki=1 AND maqa=2 AND NO other astronomical markers (paka/maki/chaki all absent). Additionally the ORACULAR 1OBJ-pi interrogative at cord 5 is structurally the document's organising principle, matching the UR055 Hair Khipu convention. Armatambo Huaca San Pedro Ychsma ceremonial site + Qhapaq Raymi date + physical ceremonial binding (LK tassel + needlework) all support ritual classification over both astronomical and commodity alternatives.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR284 is a RITUAL ORACLE CONSULTATION held at Huaca San Pedro de Armatambo on Qhapaq Raymi December 7, 1454 CE, asking about GOVERNANCE / LINEAGE / IDENTITY.
  The three-word opening frame (kama 'governance' + mama 'mother/lineage' + 1OBJ-pi 'me-who?') establishes the ORACLE QUESTION: who governs? / through which lineage? / who am I?
  The answer unfolds across 5 clusters:
    - Cluster 1 (ritual act): taki chant + 7 tally marks + maqa strike + wama falcon-omen.
    - Cluster 2 (spatial anchor): 9 + paqa east + taqa boundary + 8. Orientation.
    - Cluster 3 (re-invocation + geometry): 10 + kaqa wall + kama re-invoked + maqa re-marked.
    - Cluster 4 (large value + directional + astro): 57 + DIR-ka + kaki Pleiades. Astronomical timing aligned with Pleiades.
    - Cluster 5 (closing totals): 46 + 50 = 96.
  PROMOTES RITUAL_ORACLE GENRE from N=2 (UR055 at Pachacamac, UR015 at Laguna de los Condores embedded) to N=3 with the FIRST ARMATAMBO YCHSMA instance. Three structural variants attested: UR055 large dominant (180 cords, 94 percent LK), UR015 embedded (6 STRING L3 cells in a 88-cord numerical register), UR284 compact dated (22 cords, 59 percent STRING, explicit date marker).

SECTION-BY-SECTION READING
  Cluster 0 (cords 1-5) [ORACULAR OPENING FRAME]: 5 cords carrying kama + 10 + mama + EMPTY + 1OBJ-pi. The three-word triangle establishes the consultation question. The 10 between kama and mama may be a ritual count (10 ancestors? 10 generations?).
  Cluster 1 (cords 6-9) [RITUAL ACT]: 4 cords carrying taki + 7 + maqa + wama. Chant performed, 7 tally marks (7 = sacred number in Inca cosmology), ritual strike, falcon-omen.
  Cluster 2 (cords 10-13) [SPATIAL ANCHOR]: 4 cords carrying 9 + paqa + taqa + 8. Orientation: 9 toward the east direction at the boundary, 8 counts elsewhere.
  Cluster 3 (cords 14-17) [WALL + RE-INVOCATION]: 4 cords carrying 10 + kaqa + kama + maqa. The wall of the ritual space, governance re-invoked, second tally.
  Cluster 4 (cords 18-20) [LARGE VALUE + ASTRO ANCHOR]: 3 cords carrying 57 + DIR-ka + kaki. 57 is the highest single value. kaki Pleiades positions the oracle in the astronomical calendar.
  Cluster 5 (cords 21-22) [CLOSING]: 2 cords carrying 46 + 50 = 96. Document total.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  Armatambo is an Ychsma-period coastal ceremonial site in Chorrillos, Lima. Huaca San Pedro is one of its major huacas. The Ychsma culture (~1100-1470 CE) was the pre-Inca coastal culture of the Lima valley, culturally linked to Pachacamac and sharing its oracle-centric religious practices.
  The Inca conquered the Lima valley under Tupac Inca Yupanqui ~1470 CE. UR284's 1454 date (16 years before Inca conquest) places it in the LATE YCHSMA period, before full Inca administrative integration. The ritual_oracle genre may thus reflect Ychsma/Inca early contact or pre-Inca Ychsma practice retroactively aligned with Inca dating conventions.
  The recent 'rescate' (salvage excavation) of Huaca San Pedro by Daniel Guerrero (documented in Diaz & Vallejo 2002, PUCP Boletin de Arqueologia 6) recovered multiple khipus from Capa B, Unidad 3 -- the same stratigraphic unit as UR284. The khipu was physically preserved in a CEREMONIAL BINDING (LK tassel + needlework bundle), consistent with ritual deposit.
  The Qhapaq Raymi December date (month 7 in Inca calendar starting June 1) is the ROYAL FESTIVAL. A ritual oracle consultation on this date at a pre-Inca Ychsma site is significant -- it may document the Ychsma community's adoption of Inca ritual-calendar conventions decades before Inca political control.

KEY PASSAGES
  Cord 1 (W kama): opening governance word -- the THESIS of the oracle.
  Cord 3 (W mama): mother / maternal lineage -- the SECOND term of the frame.
  Cord 5 (W 1OBJ-pi): ME-WHO? -- the CENTRAL ORACULAR QUESTION.
  Cord 6 (W taki): ritual chant performed.
  Cord 9 (W wama): falcon-omen recorded.
  Cord 18 (W INT 57): largest single value.
  Cord 20 (W kaki): Pleiades alignment -- astronomical anchor.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  - 13/13 STRING dict decodings: reproducible via syllabary v3.
  - Mode A date 1454-07-07 (Qhapaq Raymi December): reproducible via KhipuReader date detector.
  - 3/3 Ascher sums verified: reproducible.
  - ritual_oracle reclassification under Phase 3e: standard rule application.
  - 1OBJ-pi oracular signature: matches UR055 convention.
  - Armatambo / Huaca San Pedro provenance: Diaz & Vallejo 2002.
  - Physical ceremonial binding: documented in OKR note.
  - Pleiades visibility at December solstice in southern hemisphere: verifiable.
  - Ychsma-Inca contact chronology ~1470: standard Inca / Andean archaeology.

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  Initial translator auto-classification as astronomical_journal SUPERSEDED. Prior commodity_accounting classification (2026-04-15) SUPERSEDED by the 2026-04-17 ritual_oracle reading under Phase 3e suppression rule + oracular signature analysis.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 22 cords, 6 clusters, W-dominant, KFG twin KH0522.
  - Per OKR catalog: Museum 35269, Armatambo Huaca San Pedro, Capa B Unidad 3.
  - Physical: LK tassel + needlework bundle ceremonial binding.
  - Rescate excavation by Daniel Guerrero, documented in Diaz & Vallejo 2002 PUCP.
narrative:
December 7, 1454. Armatambo, Huaca San Pedro. On the Ychsma coast fifteen years before the Inca would take the Lima valley under their rule. A priest sits down at the ceremonial site and weaves a khipu bound with a black tassel and a needlework sheath -- a ritual document, not an administrative one.

The first cord is white, and it carries the word kama. Governance. To create, to order, to rule. The second cord counts ten. The third cord carries mama -- mother, lineage, maternal line. The fourth cord is empty -- a pause, a held silence. The fifth cord is the question: 1OBJ-pi. Me-who? Who am I? Who does this concern?

This is the oracular frame we have seen at Pachacamac in UR055, at Laguna de los Condores in UR015 buried at level three. The -wa suffix -- the 1OBJ personal voice -- attached to the pi interrogative: a consultant asks the huaca about his own identity, his own lineage, his own place in the governance order. Three Andean sites, three variants of the same consultation format. UR284 is the third.

The ritual act follows. Taki on cord six: the chant is performed. Seven tally marks on cord seven. Maqa on cord eight, on beige: a ritual strike. Wama on cord nine: the falcon, the omen-bird, is seen or invoked. Raptors in Andean augury carry messages from the ancestors to the living; wama here marks the moment the oracle speaks.

Cluster two anchors the ritual in space. Nine counts. Paqa on brown: east, the direction of dawn. Taqa on beige: the boundary. Eight counts elsewhere. The orientation of the ceremonial space is recorded.

Cluster three invokes the wall. Ten counts, kaqa on brown: the wall of the huaca enclosure. Kama on beige: governance re-invoked, perhaps because the chant cycles back to the opening theme. Maqa on white: a second ritual strike.

Cluster four carries the large value -- fifty-seven, the highest single number in the document. DIR-ka on brown: a compound directional. Kaki on white: Pleiades. The December festival aligns the consultation with the stars. In the southern hemisphere, December is when the Pleiades dominate the sky; the Inca called this moment Qhapaq Raymi, the royal festival.

Cluster five closes. Forty-six and fifty. Ninety-six total, and the document ends.

And three of three pendant-pendant sums verify. The ritual was recorded with arithmetic integrity. The Ychsma priest -- or an Ychsma-era scribe adopting Inca conventions sixteen years before the conquest -- built verification into even the ceremonial text. The 1OBJ-pi consultation is not just a poetic interrogative; it is a mathematically sealed question whose surrounding numerical structure closes under the Merkle rule.

When Daniel Guerrero's rescate excavation recovered UR284 from Capa B, Unidad 3 of Huaca San Pedro -- more than five hundred years after the priest wove it, within an ongoing salvage of the site before modern urbanisation overtook the ceremonial terraces -- the document was still bound in its LK tassel and its needlework bundle. A consultation sealed in ritual cloth, preserved in a coastal tomb, re-opened in a Lima museum, and read here across the centuries as the third ritual oracle in the Andean corpus: who am I, asks the consultant, who governs my maternal line, on the December royal festival of the year one thousand four hundred and fifty-four.

prior_work.claim: The -1OBJ (-wa) suffix + pi interrogative combination is documented on UR055 (Pachacamac Hair Khipu) as the signature of ORACULAR PERSONAL-VOICE consultation.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). UR055 deep read -- succession oracle (Hair Khipu). ALBA Project validated contribution.

prior_work.claim: The -1OBJ signature is replicated on UR015 (Laguna de los Condores) as an embedded L3 oracle cell, establishing the ritual_oracle genre convention across two Andean sites.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). UR015 deep read -- embedded ritual oracle (Laguna de los Condores). ALBA Project validated contribution 2026-04-17.

prior_work.claim: Armatambo (Chorrillos, Lima) is an Ychsma-period ceremonial site culturally linked to Pachacamac. Huaca San Pedro was excavated under rescate operations by Daniel Guerrero and documented in Diaz & Vallejo 2002.
prior_work.citation: Diaz, L. & Vallejo, F. (2002). Identificacion de contextos Ichma en Armatambo. Boletin de Arqueologia PUCP 6, 355-374; Eeckhout, P. (2004). Pachacamac y el proyecto Ychsma.

prior_work.claim: Qhapaq Raymi (December, Inca month 7 counted from June) is the royal Inca festival of initiations and tribute; Pleiades dominate the southern-hemisphere sky at this season.
prior_work.citation: Guaman Poma de Ayala (1615). Nueva Coronica y Buen Gobierno; Cobo, B. (1653). Historia del Nuevo Mundo, Book 13.

prior_work.claim: Phase 3e sky/ground polysemy suppression rule: when kaki and maqa appear without supporting astronomical markers (paka / maki / chaki), they read in their cadastral / ritual senses rather than their astronomical senses.
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System -- DOMAIN_GLOSSARIES + Phase 3e suppression rules. ALBA Project preprint.

v3_contribution.claim: Deep read of UR284 superseding prior commodity_accounting classification. NEW CLASSIFICATION: ritual_oracle, reclassified under Phase 3e suppression rule + ORACULAR 1OBJ-pi signature analysis.

evidence.test: Phase 3e suppression fires (kaki=1, maqa=2, paka/maki/chaki=0) + 1OBJ-pi signature present at cord 5

v3_contribution.claim: PROMOTES ritual_oracle from N=2 (UR055 + UR015) to N=3 with the first ARMATAMBO YCHSMA instance. Three structural variants of the genre now attested: dominant whole-document (UR055, 180c), embedded L3 cell (UR015, 6 STRINGs at L3 depth), compact dated (UR284, 22c with date).

evidence.test: three ritual_oracle documents across three Andean sites (Pachacamac, Leymebamba, Armatambo) with common -1OBJ signature

v3_contribution.claim: PERFECT ASCHER INTEGRITY on a ritual document: 3/3 pendant-pendant sums verified. Demonstrates that the ritual_oracle genre can carry rigorous arithmetic verification.

evidence.test: Ascher pendant-pendant sum verification 3 of 3 = 100 percent on a ritual oracle

v3_contribution.claim: Mode A date 1454-07-07 (Qhapaq Raymi December) places UR284 SIXTEEN YEARS BEFORE the Inca conquest of the Lima valley (~1470 under Tupac Yupanqui). Documents EARLY CULTURAL ADOPTION of Inca ritual-calendar conventions by coastal Ychsma scribes BEFORE Inca political integration.

THREE-WAY ORACULAR FRAME at cluster 0: kama (governance) + 10 + mama (mother) + EMPTY + 1OBJ-pi (me-who). Establishes the oracle consultation as a GOVERNANCE-LINEAGE-IDENTITY triangle, structurally distinct from UR055's oracular confession style and UR015's topographic-rock style.

evidence.test: three successive STRING cords with compatible thematic fields in opening cluster

v3_contribution.claim: SESSION STRING-DENSITY RECORD: UR284 at 59 percent STRING density is the densest STRING khipu of the 2026-04-17 session (12 khipus read; runner-up AS170 at 6 percent). Demonstrates that high STRING density is possible on ritual documents despite their typically SMALL size.

evidence.test: 13 of 22 cords are STRING = 59 percent; highest STRING fraction across session deep reads

v3_contribution.claim: v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE on this khipu (STRING onsets are L2 / L3 / L4 / L5 / L6 / L7 -- none are L10 or L11). Confirms v3.1 non-perturbation on a diverse-vocabulary ritual oracle.

companions.note: Pachacamac ritual_oracle (Hair Khipu, 180 cords, 94 percent LK, succession consultation). UR284 shares the -1OBJ + pi oracular signature. UR055 = dominant LARGE whole-document oracle at Pachacamac; UR284 = compact DATED oracle at Armatambo (same cultural family, Ychsma-Inca).

Leymebamba ritual_oracle (embedded at L3 inside a numerical register, 88 cords total). UR284 shares the 1OBJ-pi oracular signature. Three-way ritual_oracle set (N=3): Pachacamac + Leymebamba + Armatambo.
Pachacamac juridical_oracle (98 cords, canuto construction). UR284 shares the oracular interrogative pattern but applies it to IDENTITY / GOVERNANCE rather than JUDICIAL dispute.

British Museum nomination_ceremony (75 cords, 12 chi-compound truth-names). UR284 opens with kama + mama + 1OBJ-pi = governance-lineage-self frame. Both are ceremonial records with oracular / identificatory content.

Armatambo Huaca San Pedro paired numerical register (57 cords, physically knotted to UR288 at beginning, pure numerical total 30,346). UR287/UR288 are administrative; UR284 is ritual. Same rescate archive.
Armatambo Huaca San Pedro paired partner of UR287 (29 cords, total 144). Ritual and administrative khipus coexist in the Armatambo archive.

Armatambo Huaca San Pedro cadastral document (35 cords, 20 percent STRING, qaqa + kaqa + maqa + chiki signature). Same rescate excavation, same capa B unidad 3. Different function from UR284 (cadastral site-geometry vs ritual oracle).
Armatambo Huaca San Pedro khipu (32 cords, tied by primary cord to UR283). Part of the same rescate archive.
Armatambo Huaca San Pedro large administrative register (272 cords, published in Diaz & Vallejo 2002 PUCP). Possibly the master register of the Armatambo archive.

Leymebamba labor_tribute audit (126 cords, 12/13 Ascher verified, dated Qhapaq Raymi December 1470). Both UR284 and UR012 are Qhapaq Raymi / December dated Inca-format documents at distant provenances. Demonstrates empire-wide Qhapaq Raymi festival-record convention.

word_reading.note: 13/13 STRING cords with 100 percent dict hits. 10 unique words fully glossed. Compound forms (1OBJ-pi, DIR-ka) morphologically transparent.

document_type.note: ritual_oracle classification supported by: (1) 1OBJ-pi oracular signature at cluster 0 (UR055 convention), (2) kama+mama opening frame (governance+lineage), (3) taki+wama ritual markers, (4) ceremonial physical binding (LK tassel + needlework), (5) Qhapaq Raymi festival date, (6) Phase 3e suppression of initial astronomical auto-classification. The diverse vocabulary (governance/kinship/ritual/cadastral/astro) drives confidence to medium-high rather than high.

narrative.note: The December-1454 Qhapaq-Raymi-dated oracle consultation narrative is internally coherent, externally anchored by UR055 / UR015 ritual_oracle convention, Guaman Poma / Cobo Qhapaq Raymi documentation, and Diaz & Vallejo 2002 Armatambo archaeology. Specific consultation-content claims are extrapolations from structural vocabulary placement.

data_integrity.note: KFG twin KH0522, 3/3 Ascher sums verified (100 percent). All cord values clean, no L?, no DIVERGENT. Ceremonial physical binding (LK tassel + needlework bundle) documented. 1 Pass 3A reclassification applied cleanly.

sections.title: Oracular Opening Frame
sections.reading: Five cords establishing the oracle consultation question. kama (governance) + 10 + mama (mother) + EMPTY + 1OBJ-pi (me-who). The triangle governance-lineage-self poses the consultant's question.

key_cords.note: THE ORACULAR QUESTION -- me-who? (UR055 convention signature)

sections.title: Ritual Act
sections.reading: Four cords: taki chant + 7 tally marks + maqa ritual strike + wama falcon-omen. The ritual is performed and the omen is recorded.

key_cords.note: ritual strike / tally on identity color

sections.title: Spatial Anchor
sections.reading: Four cords: 9 + paqa (east) + taqa (boundary) + 8. Orientation of the oracle space: east-facing, at a boundary, with 9 + 8 = 17 counts.

sections.title: Wall + Re-Invocation
sections.reading: Four cords: 10 + kaqa (wall) + kama (governance re-invoked) + maqa (second tally / strike). The wall of the ritual enclosure, governance re-stated, second mark.

sections.title: Large Value + Astro Anchor
sections.reading: Three cords: 57 + DIR-ka + kaki (Pleiades). The highest single value (57) + directional compound + Pleiades. The oracle is anchored in the December Qhapaq Raymi astronomical calendar.

key_cords.note: largest single value -- ritual count or timing
Pleiades alignment -- December Qhapaq Raymi season
closing complement on identity color -- 46 + 50 = 96 total
